commit.message:
fix(ui): use Activity window flag for keep-screen-on during streaming

Replace View-based keepScreenOn with Activity.window.addFlags(
FLAG_KEEP_SCREEN_ON). The View-based approach only worked while the
StreamScreen composable was visible — navigating to Settings would
clear the flag and allow the screen to lock mid-stream.

The Activity window flag survives in-app navigation since it's set on
the Activity window, not a specific View. The flag is cleared when
streaming stops, the setting is disabled, or the composable disposes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(service): add PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK to prevent CPU sleep during streaming

Acquire a PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK when streaming starts and release it when
streaming stops or the service is destroyed. This prevents the CPU from
sleeping when the app is in the background, which would stall the stream.

Uses a 4-hour safety timeout to prevent battery drain from leaked locks.
WAKE_LOCK permission was already declared in the manifest.

commit.message:
fix(jetson): match Docker container UID with host agrikd user

Root cause: Dockerfile created agrikd with UID 1000, but host's
agrikd user has UID 2010. Mounted volume files (owned by 2010,
mode 644) were inaccessible to container process (UID 1000).

Fix: Pass HOST_UID/HOST_GID as Docker build args so container user
matches host user exactly. Also chmod 666 existing files in logs/
and data/ dirs during setup to handle re-run scenarios.

commit.message:
fix: prevent UnicodeDecodeError on /errors when stored data contains invalid UTF-8 bytes (#205)

* Initial plan

* fix: sanitize non-UTF-8 bytes in ERROR_SOURCES_SQL using toValidUTF8 and mapApply

Applies toValidUTF8() to all String scalar columns (ServiceName, TraceId,
SpanId, Body) and mapApply((k,v)->(k,toValidUTF8(v))) to LogAttributes map
values in ERROR_SOURCES_SQL. This prevents chDB's sqlitelike execute from
crashing with UnicodeDecodeError when stored error data contains raw non-UTF-8
byte sequences (e.g. truncated multi-byte characters from OTLP agents).

Adds regression test: injects a row with invalid UTF-8 bytes via unhex() and
verifies both normal and grouped /errors pages return HTTP 200.

fix: security audit — SSRF protection, body forwarding, hop-by-hop filtering (#1)

## Bug fixes
- Proxy now forwards request body for POST/PUT/PATCH (was silently dropped)

- Re-registration of removed extensions now works (delete old record first)

  private/loopback/link-local/metadata IPs (RFC1918, 169.254.169.254, etc.)
- Proxy filters hop-by-hop headers (Host, Connection, Transfer-Encoding, etc.)
- Reuse shared reqwest::Client instead of creating one per request
- Webhook adds X-Convergio-Extension-Id header for traceability

## Quality
- Poisoned mutex now logged with warning instead of silently ignored
- 5 new tests: SSRF validation, scheme validation, error_body, delete+reinsert, hop-by-hop

* fix: UX improvements — -j/--json flag, TOTP visibility, search via get-logins, clip/totp by URL

  The flag can be placed anywhere after the subcommand name, which is the

  resolved via get_logins and the UUID is passed to get_totp internally.

- clip: 'field' is now a positional argument instead of --field.
  New usage: keepassxc-cli clip <password|username|totp> <url>

tui: fix help overlay ANSI code corruption and add proper line styling

The help text (HELP_TEXT_BASE) contains ANSI escape sequences (\x1b[1m,
\x1b[0m) designed for raw terminal output. When passed to ratatui's
rendering pipeline, these bytes leaked through to the terminal, causing
random style corruption (some lines white, some gray, inconsistent).

Fix:
- Add strip_ansi() to remove CSI sequences before ratatui touches the text
- Add style_info_line() for per-line ratatui styling:
  * Section headers (no indent): Cyan Bold
  * Command lines (/cmd [args]  desc): command=White Bold, desc=Muted
  * Tip lines (• ...): Muted
  * Everything else: terminal default

commit.message:
perf: fix marketplace timeout — optimise queries + raise axios timeout to 60s

Root causes of 'timeout of 30000ms exceeded' on Marketplace page:
1. listListings() used correlated subqueries (COUNT/MAX per row) — N+1 anti-pattern
2. adminListAllListings() same correlated subquery issue
3. Missing composite indexes on status+admin_approved and bids listing+status
4. Axios global timeout was 30s — free-tier Render DB can take 30-90s to wake

Fixes:
- MarketplaceService: correlated subqueries → aggregated LEFT JOIN (single scan)
- database.ts: seedDefaultSettings creates 4 perf indexes on startup (idempotent)
- api.ts + client.ts: timeout 30000 → 60000ms
- api.ts: added second interceptor to enrich timeout errors with isTimeout flag
  so pages can show 'server waking up' message instead of generic error

commit.message:
Fix manga search input swallowing keystrokes and breaking IME (#360)

* Initial plan

* Fix search input swallowing and IME interruption on manga view

- Bind MudTextField to a local `_searchInput` field instead of the
  `[Parameter]` `SearchString`, preventing URL-driven re-renders from
  resetting the native input element while the user is typing.
- Set up the debounced URL-sync subscription once in `OnInitialized`
  using a `Subject<string>`, replacing the broken `WhenAnyValue`
  subscription that was being re-created inside `GetMangas` on every
  keystroke (each one firing after 250 ms with a stale value).
- Track `_urlSearchValue` to distinguish our own `NavigateTo` calls
  from external URL changes (browser back/forward), so `OnParametersSet`
  only resets the input when the URL changes externally.
- Remove the duplicate `OnInternalInputChanged` handler from the
  `MudTextField`; `@bind-Value:after` on `OnSearchInputChanged` covers
  the table reload.

- GR fed to meter state for WebSocket broadcast

- MeterFrame extended with gr_db field

- EQ sliders per band (freq, gain, Q)
